"workout" : "dashboard" } return "dashboard" }) const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ email: "", password: "", confirmPassword: "", }) useEffect(() => { if (user && user.gender) { const activeProgram = ProgramStateManager.getActiveProgram() if (activeProgram) { setCurrentView("workout") } } }, [user]) const handleAuth = async (type: "login" | "signup") => { setIsLoading(true) setError("") try { // Validate inputs if (!formData.email || !formData.password) { throw new Error("Email and password are required") } if (type === "signup") { if (formData.password !== formData.confirmPassword) { throw new Error("Passwords do not match") } await signUp(formData.email, formData.password) } else { await signIn(formData.email, formData.password) } } catch (err) { setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : "An error occurred") } finally { setIsLoading(false) } } const handleGoogleSignIn = async () => { setIsLoading(true) setError("") try { await signInWithGoogle() // User will be redirected to Google } catch (err) { setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : "An error occurred") setIsLoading(false) } } const handleInputChange = (field: string, value: string) => { setFormData((prev) => ({ ...prev, [field]: value })) } const handleStartWorkout = () => { setCurrentView("workout") } const handleViewChange = (view: string) => { if (view === "train") { const activeProgram = ProgramStateManager.getActiveProgram() if (activeProgram) { setCurrentView("workout") return } } setCurrentView(view as any) } const handleProgramStarted = () => { setProgramKey((prev) => prev + 1) setCurrentView("workout") } const handleWorkoutComplete = () => { console.log("[v0] handleWorkoutComplete called - updating program state") ProgramStateManager.completeWorkout() setCurrentView("train") } if (authLoading) { return (

1→export interface WorkoutSet {
     2→  id: string
     3→  reps: number
     4→  weight: number
     5→  completed: boolean
     6→  restStartTime?: number
     7→  notes?: string
     8→}
     9→
    10→export interface WorkoutExercise {
    11→  id: string
    12→  exerciseId: string
    13→  exerciseName: string
    14→  targetSets: number
    15→  targetReps: string
    16→  targetRest: string
    17→  sets: WorkoutSet[]
    18→  completed: boolean
    19→  startTime?: number
    20→  endTime?: number
    21→}
    22→
    23→export interface WorkoutSession {
    24→  id: string
    25→  userId: string
    26→  programId?: string
    27→  workoutName: string
    28→  startTime: number
    29→  endTime?: number
    30→  exercises: WorkoutExercise[]
    31→  notes?: string
    32→  completed: boolean
    33→  week?: number
    34→  day?: number
    35→}
    36→
    37→export class WorkoutLogger {
    38→  private static readonly STORAGE_KEY = "liftlog_workouts"
    39→  private static readonly IN_PROGRESS_KEY = "liftlog_in_progress_workouts"
    40→
    41→  static getInProgressWorkout(week: number, day: number): WorkoutSession | null {
    42→    if (typeof window === "undefined") return null
    43→
    44→    try {
    45→      const stored = localStorage.getItem(this.IN_PROGRESS_KEY)
    46→      if (!stored) return null
    47→
    48→      const workouts: WorkoutSession[] = JSON.parse(stored)
    49→      return workouts.find((w) => w.week === week && w.day === day) || null
    50→    } catch {
    51→      return null
    52→    }
    53→  }
    54→
    55→  static getCurrentWorkout(): WorkoutSession | null {
    56→    if (typeof window === "undefined") return null
    57→
    58→    try {
    59→      const activeProgram = typeof window !== "undefined" ? this.getActiveProgram() : null
    60→      if (!activeProgram) return null
    61→
    62→      return this.getInProgressWorkout(activeProgram.currentWeek, activeProgram.currentDay)
    63→    } catch {
    64→      return null
    65→    }
    66→  }
    67→
    68→  private static getActiveProgram() {
    69→    try {
    70→      const stored = localStorage.getItem("liftlog_active_program")
    71→      return stored ? JSON.parse(stored) : null
    72→    } catch {
    73→      return null
    74→    }
    75→  }
    76→
    77→  static saveCurrentWorkout(workout: WorkoutSession): void {
    78→    if (typeof window === "undefined") return
    79→    if (!workout.week || !workout.day) return
    80→
    81→    try {
    82→      const stored = localStorage.getItem(this.IN_PROGRESS_KEY)
    83→      let workouts: WorkoutSession[] = stored ? JSON.parse(stored) : []
    84→
    85→      // Remove existing workout for this week/day
    86→      workouts = workouts.filter((w) => !(w.week === workout.week && w.day === workout.day))
    87→
    88→      // Add updated workout
    89→      workouts.push(workout)
    90→
    91→      localStorage.setItem(this.IN_PROGRESS_KEY, JSON.stringify(workouts))
    92→    } catch (error) {
    93→      console.error("Failed to save workout:", error)
    94→    }
    95→  }
    96→
    97→  static clearCurrentWorkout(week?: number, day?: number): void {
    98→    if (typeof window === "undefined") return
    99→
   100→    try {
   101→      if (week && day) {
   102→        const stored = localStorage.getItem(this.IN_PROGRESS_KEY)
   103→        if (!stored) return
   104→
   105→        let workouts: WorkoutSession[] = JSON.parse(stored)
   106→        workouts = workouts.filter((w) => !(w.week === week && w.day === day))
   107→        localStorage.setItem(this.IN_PROGRESS_KEY, JSON.stringify(workouts))
   108→      } else {
   109→        // Clear all in-progress workouts
   110→        localStorage.removeItem(this.IN_PROGRESS_KEY)
   111→      }
   112→    } catch (error) {
   113→      console.error("Failed to clear workout:", error)
   114→    }
   115→  }
   116→
   117→  static startWorkout(
   118→    workoutName: string,
   119→    exercises: {
   120→      exerciseId: string
   121→      exerciseName: string
   122→      targetSets: number
   123→      targetReps: string
   124→      targetRest: string
   125→      suggestedWeight?: number
   126→      progressionNote?: string
   127→    }[],
   128→    week?: number,
   129→    day?: number,
   130→  ): WorkoutSession {
   131→    if (week && day) {
   132→      const existing = this.getInProgressWorkout(week, day)
   133→      if (existing) {
   134→        const isCorrupted =
   135→          !existing.week || !existing.day || existing.exercises.some((ex) => !ex.sets || ex.sets.length === 0)
   136→
   137→        if (isCorrupted) {
   138→          console.log("[v0] Found corrupted workout data, clearing and creating fresh workout")
   139→          this.clearCurrentWorkout(week, day)
   140→          // Fall through to create new workout
   141→        } else {
   142→          console.log(
   143→            "[v0] Found existing workout for week",
   144→            week,
   145→            "day",
   146→            day,
   147→            "with",
   148→            existing.exercises.length,
   149→            "exercises",
   150→          )
   151→          console.log("[v0] Workout structure:", {
   152→            id: existing.id,
   153→            exerciseCount: existing.exercises.length,
   154→            exercises: existing.exercises.map((ex) => ({
   155→              name: ex.exerciseName,
   156→              setsCount: ex.sets?.length || 0,
   157→              hasSets: !!ex.sets,
   158→            })),
   159→          })
   160→          return existing
   161→        }
   162→      }
   163→    }
   164→
   165→    console.log("[v0] Creating new workout for week", week, "day", day)
   166→    const workout: WorkoutSession = {
   167→      id: Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9),
   168→      userId: "current-user",
   169→      workoutName,
   170→      startTime: Date.now(),
   171→      week,
   172→      day,
   173→      exercises: exercises.map((ex) => ({
   174→        id: Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9),
   175→        exerciseId: ex.exerciseId,
   176→        exerciseName: ex.exerciseName,
   177→        targetSets: ex.targetSets,
   178→        targetReps: ex.targetReps,
   179→        targetRest: ex.targetRest,
   180→        sets: Array.from({ length: ex.targetSets }, (_, i) => ({
   181→          id: Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9),
   182→          reps: 0,
   183→          weight: ex.suggestedWeight || 0, // Pre-fill with suggested weight from progression
   184→          completed: false,
   185→        })),
   186→        completed: false,
   187→      })),
   188→      completed: false,
   189→      notes: exercises.find((e) => e.progressionNote)?.progressionNote || "",
   190→    }
   191→
   192→    console.log("[v0] Created workout structure:", {
   193→      id: workout.id,
   194→      exerciseCount: workout.exercises.length,
   195→      exercises: workout.exercises.map((ex) => ({
   196→        name: ex.exerciseName,
   197→        setsCount: ex.sets.length,
   198→      })),
   199→    })
   200→
   201→    this.saveCurrentWorkout(workout)
   202→    console.log("[v0] Saved workout to localStorage")
   203→    return workout
   204→  }
   205→
   206→  static updateSet(
   207→    workout: WorkoutSession,
   208→    exerciseId: string,
   209→    setId: string,
   210→    updates: Partial<WorkoutSet>,
   211→  ): WorkoutSession | null {
   212→    const updatedWorkout = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(workout)) as WorkoutSession
   213→
   214→    const exercise = updatedWorkout.exercises.find((ex) => ex.id === exerciseId)
   215→    if (!exercise) return null
   216→
   217→    const set = exercise.sets.find((s) => s.id === setId)
   218→    if (!set) return null
   219→
   220→    Object.assign(set, updates)
   221→
   222→    // Check if exercise is completed
   223→    exercise.completed = exercise.sets.every((s) => s.completed)
   224→    if (exercise.completed && !exercise.endTime) {
   225→      exercise.endTime = Date.now()
   226→    }
   227→
   228→    this.saveCurrentWorkout(updatedWorkout)
   229→    return updatedWorkout
   230→  }
   231→
   232→  static startRest(workoutId: string, exerciseId: string, setId: string): WorkoutSession | null {
   233→    const workout = this.getCurrentWorkout()
   234→    if (!workout || workout.id !== workoutId) return null
   235→
   236→    const exercise = workout.exercises.find((ex) => ex.id === exerciseId)
   237→    if (!exercise) return null
   238→
   239→    const set = exercise.sets.find((s) => s.id === setId)
   240→    if (!set) return null
   241→
   242→    set.restStartTime = Date.now()
   243→    this.saveCurrentWorkout(workout)
   244→    return workout
   245→  }
   246→
   247→  static completeWorkout(workoutId: string): WorkoutSession | null {
   248→    if (typeof window === "undefined") return null
   249→
   250→    try {
   251→      const stored = localStorage.getItem(this.IN_PROGRESS_KEY)
   252→      if (!stored) return null
   253→
   254→      const workouts: WorkoutSession[] = JSON.parse(stored)
   255→      const workout = workouts.find((w) => w.id === workoutId)
   256→      if (!workout) return null
   257→
   258→      workout.completed = true
   259→      workout.endTime = Date.now()
   260→
   261→      // Save to workout history
   262→      this.saveWorkoutToHistory(workout)
   263→
   264→      // Remove from in-progress
   265→      if (workout.week && workout.day) {
   266→        this.clearCurrentWorkout(workout.week, workout.day)
   267→      }
   268→
   269→      return workout
   270→    } catch (error) {
   271→      console.error("Failed to complete workout:", error)
   272→      return null
   273→    }
   274→  }
   275→
   276→  private static saveWorkoutToHistory(workout: WorkoutSession): void {
   277→    if (typeof window === "undefined") return
   278→
   279→    try {
   280→      const existing = localStorage.getItem(this.STORAGE_KEY)
   281→      const workouts: WorkoutSession[] = existing ? JSON.parse(existing) : []
   282→      workouts.push(workout)
   283→      localStorage.setItem(this.STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(workouts))
   284→    } catch (error) {
   285→      console.error("Failed to save workout to history:", error)
   286→    }
   287→  }
   288→
   289→  static getWorkoutHistory(): WorkoutSession[] {
   290→    if (typeof window === "undefined") return []
   291→
   292→    try {
   293→      const stored = localStorage.getItem(this.STORAGE_KEY)
   294→      return stored ? JSON.parse(stored) : []
   295→    } catch {
   296→      return []
   297→    }
   298→  }
   299→
   300→  static getCompletedWorkout(week: number, day: number): WorkoutSession | null {
   301→    const history = this.getWorkoutHistory()
   302→    return history.find((workout) => workout.week === week && workout.day === day && workout.completed) || null
   303→  }
   304→
   305→  static getWorkout(week: number, day: number): WorkoutSession | null {
   306→    // First check completed workouts in history
   307→    const completed = this.getCompletedWorkout(week, day)
   308→    if (completed) return completed
   309→
   310→    // Then check in-progress workouts
   311→    return this.getInProgressWorkout(week, day)
   312→  }
   313→
   314→  static hasCompletedWorkout(week: number, day: number): boolean {
   315→    const history = this.getWorkoutHistory()
   316→    return history.some((workout) => workout.week === week && workout.day === day && workout.completed)
   317→  }
   318→
   319→  static isWeekCompleted(week: number, daysPerWeek: number): boolean {
   320→    for (let day = 1; day <= daysPerWeek; day++) {
   321→      if (!this.hasCompletedWorkout(week, day)) {
   322→        return false
   323→      }
   324→    }
   325→    return true
   326→  }
   327→}
   328→

1→"use client"
     2→
     3→import { useState, useEffect } from "react"
     4→import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"
     5→import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle } from "@/components/ui/card"
     6→import { Input } from "@/components/ui/input"
     7→import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge"

     9→import { Textarea } from "@/components/ui/textarea"
    10→import {

content.text:
Perfect! I've completed my analysis. Let me present my findings and the plan to fix all inconsistencies.

## Analysis Summary

I found **7 major inconsistencies** across the project:

### 🔴 **Critical Issues Found:**

1. **Navigation Inconsistency - Sidebar Missing from Analytics View**
   - **Location**: [app/page.tsx:201-207](app/page.tsx#L201-207)
   - **Problem**: Analytics view doesn't have `SidebarNavigation`, but Train, Profile, Programs, and Workout views do
   - **Impact**: User gets stuck on desktop view when navigating to Analytics

2. **Navigation ID Mismatch - Sidebar vs Views**
   - **Location**: [components/sidebar-navigation.tsx:15](components/sidebar-navigation.tsx#L15) and [components/bottom-navigation.tsx:13](components/bottom-navigation.tsx#L13)
   - **Problem**: 
     - Sidebar has: `workout`, `programs`, `exercises`, `templates`
     - Bottom nav has: `train`, `programs`, `analytics`, `profile`
     - App views support: `train`, `programs`, `workout`, `analytics`, `profile`
   - **Impact**: Clicking sidebar items like "exercises" or "templates" does nothing; clicking "Current workout" doesn't work properly

3. **Navigation Logic Inconsistency - Train Button Behavior**
   - **Location**: [app/page.tsx:98-107](app/page.tsx#L98-107)
   - **Problem**: `handleViewChange` intercepts "train" click and redirects to "workout" if there's an active program
   - **Impact**: User can't access Train view when they have an active program (they get sent to workout instead)

4. **Initial View Logic Bug**
   - **Location**: [app/page.tsx:27-35](app/page.tsx#L27-35) and [app/page.tsx:43-50](app/page.tsx#L43-50)
   - **Problem**: If user has active program, initial view is set to "workout" on page load
   - **Impact**: User always lands on workout view instead of Train tab, even if they want to see program overview

5. **Ghost Program Data - Stale In-Progress Workouts**
   - **Location**: [components/workout-logger.tsx:136-172](components/workout-logger.tsx#L136-172)
   - **Problem**: Cleanup logic exists but only runs in workout-logger component mount
   - **Impact**: Empty in-progress workouts can persist after completion, causing confusion

6. **Inconsistent View Names**
   - **Problem**: Dashboard view exists in state types but isn't used anywhere
   - **Impact**: Code confusion and potential bugs

7. **localStorage Key Consistency**
   - **Status**: ✅ **Already Fixed** - All code correctly uses `liftlog_active_program`

input.plan:
## Fix Navigation & Ghost Program Inconsistencies

### Changes to make:

1. **Add SidebarNavigation to Analytics view** ([app/page.tsx:201-207](app/page.tsx#L201-207))
   - Match layout structure of Train/Profile/Programs views

2. **Fix Sidebar navigation items** ([components/sidebar-navigation.tsx:14-19](components/sidebar-navigation.tsx#L14-19))
   - Replace: `workout` → `train`, remove `exercises` and `templates` (not implemented yet)
   - Keep only: train, programs, analytics, profile

3. **Remove Train→Workout redirect logic** ([app/page.tsx:98-107](app/page.tsx#L98-107))
   - Let users access Train view even with active program
   - Train view already handles showing current workout properly

4. **Fix initial view logic** ([app/page.tsx:27-50](app/page.tsx#L27-50))
   - Always start on "train" view instead of "workout"
   - Remove automatic redirect to workout on mount

5. **Move ghost workout cleanup to program-state.ts**
   - Create global cleanup function that runs on app init
   - Remove redundant cleanup from workout-logger component

6. **Remove unused "dashboard" view type**
   - Simplify view type to only: train, programs, workout, analytics, profile

Perfect! All tasks complete. Let me create a summary of the changes:

## ✅ All Fixes Complete

I've successfully fixed all 6 navigation and ghost program inconsistencies:

### **Changes Made:**

1. **✅ Added SidebarNavigation to Analytics view** ([app/page.tsx:201-212](app/page.tsx#L201-212))
   - Analytics now has consistent layout with Train, Profile, Programs, and Workout views
   - No more getting stuck on desktop view

2. **✅ Fixed Sidebar navigation items** ([components/sidebar-navigation.tsx:14-18](components/sidebar-navigation.tsx#L14-18))
   - Changed: `workout` → `train`, removed `exercises` and `templates`
   - Now matches: **train, programs, analytics** (+ profile in settings section)
   - Aligned with bottom navigation and actual app views

3. **✅ Removed Train→Workout redirect** ([app/page.tsx:98-100](app/page.tsx#L98-100))
   - Users can now access Train view even with active program
   - Simplified navigation logic

4. **✅ Fixed initial view** ([app/page.tsx:27](app/page.tsx#L27))
   - App always starts on **"train"** view now
   - Removed automatic redirect to workout on mount
   - Removed unused **"dashboard"** view type from state

5. **✅ Centralized ghost workout cleanup** ([lib/program-state.ts:280-329](lib/program-state.ts#L280-329))
   - Created `ProgramStateManager.cleanupGhostWorkouts()` method
   - Runs once on app initialization ([app/page.tsx:35-38](app/page.tsx#L35-38))
   - Removed duplicate cleanup from workout-logger component

### **What This Fixes:**

- ✅ Sidebar appears consistently across all views (including Analytics)
- ✅ All navigation buttons work correctly (no dead buttons)
- ✅ Users can access Train view anytime (not forced into workout)
- ✅ App always starts on Train tab (consistent UX)
- ✅ Ghost/duplicate in-progress workouts are cleaned up automatically
- ✅ Cleaner codebase with no unused view types

"use client"

import { useState } from "react"
import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"
import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle } from "@/components/ui/card"
import { Input } from "@/components/ui/input"
import { Label } from "@/components/ui/label"
import { RadioGroup, RadioGroupItem } from "@/components/ui/radio-group"
import { Checkbox } from "@/components/ui/checkbox"
import { Avatar, AvatarFallback } from "@/components/ui/avatar"
import { Separator } from "@/components/ui/separator"
import { useAuth } from "@/contexts/auth-context"
import { User, Mail, Target, Award, LogOut, Edit2, Check, X } from "lucide-react"
import type { User as UserType } from "@/lib/auth"

const MALE_GOALS = [
  "Build muscle mass",
  "Increase strength",
  "Lose fat",
  "Improve athletic performance",
  "General fitness",
  "Powerlifting",
  "Bodybuilding",
]

const FEMALE_GOALS = [
  "Tone and sculpt",
  "Build lean muscle",
  "Lose weight",
  "Improve strength",
  "General fitness",
  "Athletic performance",
  "Postural improvement",
]

export function ProfileSection() {
  const { user, updateUser, signOut } = useAuth()
  const [isEditing, setIsEditing] = useState(false)
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({
    name: user?.name || "",
    gender: user?.gender || "",
    experience: user?.experience || "",
    goals: user?.goals || [],
  })

  if (!user) return null

  const handleSave = () => {
    updateUser({
      name: formData.name,
      gender: formData.gender as "male" | "female" | "Prefer not say",
      experience: formData.experience as "beginner" | "intermediate" | "advanced",
      goals: formData.goals,
    })
    setIsEditing(false)
  }

  const handleCancel = () => {
    setFormData({
      name: user?.name || "",
      gender: user?.gender || "",
      experience: user?.experience || "",
      goals: user?.goals || [],
    })
    setIsEditing(false)
  }

  const handleGoalToggle = (goal: string) => {
    setFormData((prev) => ({
      ...prev,
      goals: prev.goals.includes(goal) ? prev.goals.filter((g) => g !== goal) : [...prev.goals, goal],
    }))
  }

  const getInitials = (name: string) => {
    return name
      .split(" ")
      .map((n) => n[0])
      .join("")
      .toUpperCase()

content.text:
Perfect! ✅ All three sections are now responsive. Here's what I fixed:

## Fixed Responsive Layouts

**Problem**: Train, Analytics, and Profile sections were locked to `max-w-md` (448px), making them look mobile-only on desktop.

**Solution**: Added responsive max-width breakpoints using Tailwind's `lg:` prefix:

1. **Train Section** ([components/train-section.tsx](components/train-section.tsx))
   - Changed: `max-w-md` → `max-w-md lg:max-w-2xl` (768px on desktop)
   - Applied to both empty state and active program views

2. **Analytics Section** ([components/analytics-section.tsx](components/analytics-section.tsx))
   - Changed: `max-w-md` → `max-w-md lg:max-w-4xl` (896px on desktop)
   - Wider layout for better data visualization

3. **Profile Section** ([components/profile-section.tsx](components/profile-section.tsx))
   - Changed: `max-w-2xl` → `max-w-md lg:max-w-3xl` (768px on desktop)
   - Consistent mobile-first approach

Now all sections will:
- Stay mobile-optimized on small screens (`max-w-md` = 448px)
- Expand appropriately on desktop (`lg:` breakpoint at 1024px+)
- Match the responsive behavior of the Programs section

content.text:
I see the problem! The blocking logic on **line 171** is comparing `existingWorkout.week > week`, which checks if you're trying to access a workout from a future week. But the issue is:

1. You completed W1D1
2. The program's `currentWeek` is still 1 (because not all days in Week 1 are complete)
3. When you try to navigate to W2D1, it's checking if `existingWorkout.week (2) > currentWeek (1)` → TRUE
4. Then it checks if Week 1 is complete → FALSE (you only did Day 1)
5. So it blocks W2D1

**The problem**: The original design expected you to complete ALL days in a week before moving to the next week. But you want to be able to jump to W2D1 after just completing W1D1.

Let me check what the intended behavior should be. Looking at your comment "Now it should open since I finished Week 1 Day 1" - it sounds like you want users to be able to access any workout in any week once they've started the program. Is that correct?

Let me look at the calendar/navigation to understand how users select workouts:

content.text:
I found it! The blocking logic at **line 554-570** is correct - it allows any day in the current week and blocks future weeks until the current week is complete. The problem is in the initial load logic at **line 171-192** which has outdated blocking logic.

The issue: When the component loads initially with an existing workout (line 146-192), it's using old blocking logic that compares the workout week to the active program's current week. But you completed W1D1, so you should be able to access W2D1 because that's just moving forward.

**The real question is**: Should users be able to freely jump between weeks, or should they complete Week 1 entirely before accessing Week 2?

Based on the comment at line 552: "Allow current week (any day order) + completed weeks. Block future weeks until current week is fully completed" - it seems the intended behavior is to complete ALL days in Week 1 before accessing Week 2.

But you want to access W2D1 after just completing W1D1. This means you want a more flexible progression system. Let me clarify - should the progression be:
1. **Linear**: W1D1 → W1D2 → W1D3 → W2D1 (current system)
2. **Free**: Any day in any week unlocked once you start the program

Which behavior do you want?

The idea is going to make sense when we start working on the progression system. 

Yes, it's correct that the user must finish the entire current week. However If I completed and logged Week 1 and Day 1, Then I can navigate and see what my next week target goals are for Week 2 Day 1. However I will NOT be able to log week 2 until entire week 1 is logged. 

It's more for visual feedback of what to expect next week based on current week, but I can't log it until the entire current week is over.

Ah, I understand now! You want to **view** W2D1 to see the progression targets (what weights you should aim for next week), but not be able to **log/edit** it until W1 is complete. This is perfect for planning and motivation!

content.text:
Perfect! ✅ All done! Here's what I implemented:

## View-Only Preview Mode for Future Weeks

**What changed:**

1. **✅ Added View-Only Mode** ([components/workout-logger.tsx:85](components/workout-logger.tsx#L85))
   - New state: `isViewOnlyMode` to distinguish between "locked" and "preview"

2. **✅ Updated Blocking Logic** (lines 172-196, 557-576)
   - **Old**: Blocked access to future weeks entirely
   - **New**: Allow viewing future weeks, but prevent editing/logging
   - When W1 incomplete and viewing W2D1:
     - `isWorkoutBlocked` = false (no blocking overlay)
     - `isViewOnlyMode` = true (inputs disabled)
     - Shows helpful message

3. **✅ Disabled Inputs in View-Only Mode** (lines 973, 986, 1002)
   - Weight input: `disabled={isViewOnlyMode}`
   - Reps input: `disabled={isViewOnlyMode}`
   - Complete button: `disabled={isViewOnlyMode}`

4. **✅ Added Preview Banner** (lines 716-727)
   - Amber/yellow banner at top shows "Preview Mode"
   - Lock icon + clear message: "Complete all Week X workouts to log this workout"
   - User-friendly explanation

5. **✅ Updated Finish Workout Button** (line 1056-1059)
   - Disabled when in view-only mode
   - Shows message: "Preview Mode - Cannot Log"

### How it works now:

**Scenario: You completed W1D1, navigating to W2D1**

1. ✅ **You CAN view** W2D1 workout structure
2. ✅ **You CAN see** progressed weight targets (e.g., "+5lbs from last week")
3. ❌ **You CANNOT edit** weight/reps inputs (disabled)
4. ❌ **You CANNOT log** sets as complete (button disabled)
5. ❌ **You CANNOT finish** the workout (button shows "Preview Mode - Cannot Log")

**Once you complete all of Week 1:**
- W2D1 becomes fully editable
- All inputs enabled
- Can log and complete workout normally

This gives you perfect **visual feedback** for planning while maintaining **progression integrity**! 🎯
We can remove the top banner which says Week 2, Day1 : Same as last week (140lbs), because its not clear which 140lbs its referring to

export interface ProgramHistoryEntry {
  id: string
  templateId: string
  name: string
  startDate: string
  completionRate: number
  totalWorkouts: number
  completedWorkouts: number
  isActive: boolean
  endDate?: string
}

export class ProgramStateManager {
  private static readonly ACTIVE_PROGRAM_KEY = "liftlog_active_program"
  private static readonly PROGRAM_PROGRESS_KEY = "liftlog_program_progress"

input.plan:
# Plan: Enhanced Workout Menu with End Workout & End Program

## Current State Analysis
- ✅ 3-button header exists (Calendar, MoreVertical dropdown)
- ✅ Dropdown has: Workout Notes, Summary, Add Exercise, End Program
- ⚠️ **Missing**: "End Workout" option
- ⚠️ **Incomplete**: "End Program" doesn't properly handle program termination status
- ⚠️ **Missing**: Visual distinction for "ended" vs "completed" programs in History

## Changes to Implement

### 1. Add "End Workout" to Dropdown Menu
**File**: `components/workout-logger.tsx` (~line 680)
- Add new menu item between "Add Exercise" and "End Program"
- Create `handleEndWorkout()` function
- Create `showEndWorkoutDialog` state + dialog UI

### 2. Implement "End Workout" Logic
**File**: `components/workout-logger.tsx` (~line 355)
- Mark all incomplete sets as skipped (blue check)
- Set `set.completed = true`, `set.reps = 0`, `set.weight = 0`
- Save workout to history
- Return to Train view
- **Different from "End Program"**: Only ends current workout, program continues

### 3. Extend WorkoutSet Interface for Skipped Tracking
**File**: `lib/workout-logger.ts` (line 1-8)
- Add optional `skipped?: boolean` field to `WorkoutSet` interface
- This allows UI to distinguish green checks (completed) from blue checks (skipped)

### 4. Update Visual Display for Skipped Sets
**File**: `components/workout-logger.tsx` (~line 990-1010)
- Check `set.skipped` flag in addition to completed status
- Blue styling: `set.completed && (set.reps === 0 && set.weight === 0 || set.skipped)`
- Keep existing Minus icon for blue (skipped) state

### 5. Add "endedEarly" Status to ProgramHistoryEntry
**File**: `lib/program-state.ts` (line 29-39)
```typescript
export interface ProgramHistoryEntry {
  // ... existing fields
  endedEarly?: boolean  // NEW: true if user ended program before completion
  endReason?: string    // NEW: optional reason (e.g., "User ended program")
}
```

### 6. Create `endProgramEarly()` Method
**File**: `lib/program-state.ts` (new method after `completeWorkout()`)
- Mark active program's history entry with `endedEarly: true`
- Set `endDate` to current date
- Set `isActive: false`
- Clear active program from localStorage
- Clear any in-progress workouts
- Dispatch `programChanged` event

### 7. Update "End Program" Handler
**File**: `components/workout-logger.tsx` (~line 355-379)
- After marking current workout complete
- Call `ProgramStateManager.endProgramEarly()`
- Show completion dialog with proper messaging
- Navigate back to Train view

### 8. Update History Display in Programs Section
**File**: `components/programs-section.tsx` (~line 389-409)
- Check `entry.endedEarly` flag
- Show different badge/icon for ended programs:
  - ✅ Green check (100% completion)
  - ⚠️ Orange/Yellow badge "ENDED" (endedEarly = true, <100%)
  - 📊 Gray progress indicator (in progress or incomplete)
- Update text to show "Ended early" when applicable

### 9. Create End Workout Dialog
**File**: `components/workout-logger.tsx` (new dialog after End Program dialog)
- Warning message: "This will mark remaining sets as skipped and end the workout"
- No typing confirmation needed (simpler than End Program)
- Cancel and "End Workout" buttons

### 10. Program Summary Dialog Enhancement
**File**: `components/workout-logger.tsx` (~line 381-404)
- Already exists with `getWorkoutSummary()`
- Ensure it displays:
  - Total program progress (weeks completed, workouts done)
  - Current week/day position
  - Overall completion percentage
  - Completed vs skipped sets in current workout

## Summary of Dropdown Menu Items (Final State)
1. 📝 **Workout Notes** - Add personal notes to workout
2. 📊 **Program Summary** - View progress to date
3. ➕ **Add Exercise** - Add custom exercise to workout
4. ⏹️ **End Workout** (NEW) - Skip remaining sets, end this workout only
5. 🚫 **End Program** - Terminate entire program early

## Key Behaviors
- **End Workout**: Blue checks on skipped sets, workout saved to history, program continues
- **End Program**: Marks program as "ended early" in history, clears active program, all remaining sets skipped
- **History Display**: Visual distinction between completed (green), ended early (orange), and in progress (gray)

   333→  /**
   334→   * End the active program early (before completion)
   335→   * Marks program as ended in history and clears active program
   336→   */
   337→  static endProgramEarly(reason: string = "User ended program"): void {
   338→    if (typeof window === "undefined") return
   339→
   340→    const activeProgram = this.getActiveProgram()
   341→    if (!activeProgram) return
   342→
   343→    // Update history entry to mark as ended early
   344→    const history = this.getProgramHistory()
   345→    const historyEntry = history.find((p) => p.isActive)
   346→
   347→    if (historyEntry) {
   348→      historyEntry.isActive = false
   349→      historyEntry.endDate = new Date().toISOString()
   350→      historyEntry.endedEarly = true
   351→      historyEntry.endReason = reason
   352→      localStorage.setItem(this.PROGRAM_HISTORY_KEY, JSON.stringify(history))
   353→    }
   354→
   355→    // Clear active program
   356→    localStorage.removeItem(this.ACTIVE_PROGRAM_KEY)
   357→
   358→    // Clear all in-progress workouts
   359→    localStorage.removeItem("liftlog_in_progress_workouts")
   360→
   361→    window.dispatchEvent(new Event("programChanged"))
   362→
   363→    console.log("[v0] Program ended early:", reason)
   364→  }
   365→}
   366→

content.text:
Perfect! ✅ All features implemented! Here's a comprehensive summary:

## 🎉 Complete Implementation Summary

### **1. Enhanced Dropdown Menu (3 buttons → 5 actions)**
**Location**: [components/workout-logger.tsx:668-695](components/workout-logger.tsx#L668-695)

New menu items:
- 📝 **Workout Notes** - Add personal notes
- 📊 **Program Summary** - View progress to date
- ➕ **Add Exercise** - Add custom exercise (placeholder)
- ⏹️ **End Workout** (NEW) - Skip remaining sets, end current workout only
- 🚫 **End Program** (NEW BEHAVIOR) - Terminate entire program early

### **2. End Workout Feature**
**Files**: [components/workout-logger.tsx:356-382](components/workout-logger.tsx#L356-382), [864-897](components/workout-logger.tsx#L864-897)

- **Dialog**: Orange-themed warning dialog with clear messaging
- **Behavior**: 
  - Marks all incomplete sets as `skipped: true`
  - Sets `reps: 0, weight: 0, completed: true`
  - Saves workout to history
  - **Program continues** - only this workout ends
- **Visual**: Blue check (Minus icon) for skipped sets

### **3. End Program Feature (Enhanced)**
**Files**: [components/workout-logger.tsx:384-421](components/workout-logger.tsx#L384-421), [lib/program-state.ts:337-364](lib/program-state.ts#L337-364)

- **New Method**: `ProgramStateManager.endProgramEarly(reason)`
- **Behavior**:
  - Marks current workout sets as skipped
  - Updates history entry with `endedEarly: true, endReason: "User ended program"`
  - Clears active program
  - Clears all in-progress workouts
  - Navigates back to Train view after 2 seconds

### **4. Skipped Sets Visual (Blue Check)**
**Files**: [lib/workout-logger.ts:6](lib/workout-logger.ts#L6), [components/workout-logger.tsx:1078-1102](components/workout-logger.tsx#L1078-1102)

- **Interface**: Added `skipped?: boolean` to `WorkoutSet`
- **Display Logic**: 
  - Green check ✅ = Completed with weight/reps
  - Blue check ➖ = Skipped or completed with 0/0
  - Empty □ = Not yet completed
- **Styling**: `bg-blue-50 border-blue-500` for skipped

### **5. Program History Display (Status Badges)**
**File**: [components/programs-section.tsx:390-421](components/programs-section.tsx#L390-421)

Enhanced history entries with:
- ✅ **Green circle** (Check icon) - 100% completed programs
- ⚠️ **Orange circle** (AlertTriangle icon) - Programs ended early
- 🏷️ **"ENDED" badge** - Shows on program name
- 📝 **End reason** - Displays "User ended program" or custom reason
- No icon for in-progress or incomplete programs

### **How It Works:**

**Scenario 1: End Workout**
```
User clicks: End Workout
→ Dialog shows: "Your program will continue"
→ Remaining sets marked skipped (blue checks)
→ Workout saved to history
→ Returns to Train view
→ Program still active, can continue next workout
```

**Scenario 2: End Program**
```
User clicks: End Program
→ Dialog shows: Type "End Program" to confirm
→ Current workout sets marked skipped (blue checks)
→ Program marked as "ended early" in history
→ All in-progress workouts cleared
→ Returns to Train view
→ Programs > History tab shows orange badge "ENDED"
```

### **Visual Indicators:**

| State | Indicator | Icon | Color |
|-------|-----------|------|-------|
| Completed | Green check | ✅ | Green |
| Skipped | Blue minus | ➖ | Blue |
| Not done | Empty box | □ | Gray |
| Program completed | Circle + check | ✅ | Green |
| Program ended early | Circle + warning | ⚠️ | Orange |

All features are now fully functional! 🚀

When I click on finish workout. I want to see something like this. Currently it goes to a new screen and says start workout. Instead I want it to be a popup, and you can still see the background workouts.
I'll analyze the current implementation and create a plan to change the workout completion flow from a full-screen dialog to a popup overlay.

# Plan: Keep Workout Background Visible on Completion Dialog

## Current Behavior Analysis
- ✅ Workout completion uses `WorkoutCompletionDialog` component
- ✅ It's already a dialog overlay (not a new screen)
- ❌ **Problem**: Dialog has semi-transparent overlay (`bg-black/50`) that dims the background
- ❌ **Problem**: User perceives it as a "new screen" because workout exercises are hidden
- ❌ **Issue**: From screenshot, user expects to see completed workout exercises with green checkmarks in background

## Root Cause
The issue is that the **Dialog overlay** (`DialogOverlay` component in `ui/dialog.tsx`) has `bg-black/50` which creates a 50% black overlay that obscures the background workout list.

## Proposed Solution

### Option 1: Reduce Overlay Opacity (Recommended)
**Changes Required:**
1. **Update `DialogOverlay` opacity** in `workout-completion-dialog.tsx`

content.text:
Perfect! I've analyzed the current implementation. The issue is that the workout completion dialog navigates away from the workout view. Based on your screenshot, you want:

1. **Keep the workout exercises visible in the background** (blurred/dimmed)
2. **Show a centered popup overlay** with completion stats
3. **"NEXT WORKOUT"** button (red) instead of navigating away
Now I understand the current implementation and what needs to change. Let me create a comprehensive plan:

input.plan:
# Plan: Keep Workout Background Visible on Completion Dialog

## Current Behavior Analysis
- ✅ Workout completion uses `WorkoutCompletionDialog` component
- ✅ It's already a dialog overlay (not a new screen)
- ❌ **Problem**: Dialog has semi-transparent overlay (`bg-black/50`) that dims the background
- ❌ **Problem**: User perceives it as a "new screen" because workout exercises are hidden
- ❌ **Issue**: From screenshot, user expects to see completed workout exercises with green checkmarks in background

## Root Cause
The issue is that the **Dialog overlay** (`DialogOverlay` component in `ui/dialog.tsx`) has `bg-black/50` which creates a 50% black overlay that obscures the background workout list.

## Proposed Solution

### Option 1: Reduce Overlay Opacity (Recommended)
**Changes Required:**
1. **Update `DialogOverlay` opacity** in `workout-completion-dialog.tsx`

content.content: Yes, but instead of red, just keep it blue like the app color for the button

content.text: I see - the user wants the "NEXT WORKOUT" button to be blue (matching the app's primary color) instead of red. Let me update my plan:

input.plan:
# Plan: Improve Workout Completion Dialog with Visible Background

## Current Issues
1. ❌ Dialog overlay (`bg-black/50`) makes background too dark, feels like a new screen
2. ❌ User can't see their completed workout exercises with green checkmarks behind dialog
3. ❌ "NEXT WORKOUT" button is red in screenshot, should be blue (app primary color)

    22→interface WorkoutCompletionDialogProps {

    30→export function WorkoutCompletionDialog({
    31→  workout,

    35→  onStartNextWorkout,
    36→}: WorkoutCompletionDialogProps) {
    37→  const [showCelebration, setShowCelebration] = useState(false)
    38→
    39→  useEffect(() => {
    40→    if (open && workout) {
    41→      setShowCelebration(true)
    42→      const timer = setTimeout(() => setShowCelebration(false), 2000)
    43→      return () => clearTimeout(timer)
    44→    }
    45→  }, [open, workout])
    46→
    47→  if (!workout) return null
    48→
    49→  const getWorkoutStats = () => {
    50→    let totalSets = 0
    51→    let completedSets = 0
    52→    let skippedSets = 0
    53→    let totalVolume = 0
    54→    let totalReps = 0
    55→
    56→    workout.exercises.forEach((exercise) => {
    57→      exercise.sets.forEach((set) => {
    58→        totalSets++
    59→        if (set.completed) {
    60→          if (set.reps === 0 && set.weight === 0) {
    61→            skippedSets++
    62→          } else {
    63→            completedSets++
    64→            totalVolume += set.weight * set.reps
    65→            totalReps += set.reps
    66→          }
    67→        }
    68→      })
    69→    })
    70→
    71→    const duration = workout.endTime ? Math.floor((workout.endTime - workout.startTime) / 1000 / 60) : 0
    72→    const completionRate = totalSets > 0 ? Math.round((completedSets / totalSets) * 100) : 0
    73→
    74→    return {
    75→      totalSets,
    76→      completedSets,
    77→      skippedSets,
    78→      totalVolume,
    79→      totalReps,
    80→      duration,
    81→      completionRate,
    82→      exercises: workout.exercises.length,
    83→    }
    84→  }
    85→
    86→  const stats = getWorkoutStats()
    87→
    88→  const getCompletionMessage = () => {
    89→    if (stats.completionRate >= 90) return "Outstanding workout! 🔥"
    90→    if (stats.completionRate >= 75) return "Great job! 💪"
    91→    if (stats.completionRate >= 50) return "Good effort! 👍"
    92→    return "Every workout counts! 💯"
    93→  }
    94→
    95→  const getCompletionColor = () => {
    96→    if (stats.completionRate >= 90) return "text-green-600"
    97→    if (stats.completionRate >= 75) return "text-blue-600"
    98→    if (stats.completionRate >= 50) return "text-orange-600"
    99→    return "text-purple-600"
   100→  }
   101→
   102→  const handleNextWorkout = () => {
   103→    // The program state is now updated in page.tsx's handleWorkoutComplete
